examples.title: Digital Signal Encoding Simulation
examples.description: Simulates different digital signal encoding schemes (NRZ-L, NRZ-I, Manchester, Differential Manchester) used in transmission media
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates four common digital signal encoding schemes used in transmission media. NRZ-L uses voltage levels directly, NRZ-I inverts on 1s, Manchester transitions in middle of bit (self-clocking), and Differential Manchester uses transitions to encode data. Each has different characteristics for synchronization and DC balance.

examples.title: Transmission Media Properties Calculator
examples.description: Calculates propagation delay, transmission time, and bandwidth-delay product for different transmission media types
examples.code:
class TransmissionMedia:
    def __init__(self, name, speed, bandwidth_mhz, attenuation_db_per_100m):
        self.name = name
        self.speed = speed  # Speed of signal (m/s), typically 2e8 for guided media
        self.bandwidth = bandwidth_mhz * 1e6  # Convert MHz to Hz
        self.attenuation = attenuation_db_per_100m  # dB per 100 meters

    def propagation_delay(self, distance_meters):
        """Calculate propagation delay in milliseconds"""
        return (distance_meters / self.speed) * 1000

    def transmission_time(self, data_size_bits):
        """Calculate transmission time in milliseconds"""
        return (data_size_bits / self.bandwidth) * 1000

    def bandwidth_delay_product(self, distance_meters):
        """Calculate bandwidth-delay product in bits"""
        return self.bandwidth * (distance_meters / self.speed)

    def signal_strength(self, distance_meters, initial_power_dbm):
        """Calculate signal strength after attenuation (dBm)"""
        distance_100m = distance_meters / 100
        return initial_power_dbm - (self.attenuation * distance_100m)

# Define transmission media
media_types = [
    TransmissionMedia("Twisted Pair (Cat6)", 2.0e8, 250, 3.5),
    TransmissionMedia("Coaxial Cable", 2.0e8, 400, 2.0),
    TransmissionMedia("Fiber Optic (Multi)", 2.0e8, 500, 0.5),
    TransmissionMedia("Fiber Optic (Single)", 2.0e8, 10000, 0.2),
]

# Calculate for 10 km distance, 10 MB data
distance = 10000  # meters
data_size = 10 * 8 * 1024 * 1024  # 10 MB in bits

print(f"Distance: {distance/1000} km | Data: 10 MB\n")
print(f"{'Media':<22} {'Prop Delay':<12} {'Tx Time':<12} {'BDP (Mb)':<12} {'Signal@10km'}")
print("-" * 75)

for media in media_types:
    prop_delay = media.propagation_delay(distance)
    tx_time = media.transmission_time(data_size)
    bdp = media.bandwidth_delay_product(distance) / 1e6  # Convert to Mb
    signal = media.signal_strength(distance, 0)  # 0 dBm input
    print(f"{media.name:<22} {prop_delay:>8.2f} ms {tx_time:>10.2f} ms {bdp:>10.2f}  {signal:>8.1f} dBm")
examples.explanation: This code simulates transmission media properties. It calculates propagation delay (how long signal takes to travel), transmission time (how long to send data), bandwidth-delay product (pipe size), and signal attenuation (signal loss over distance). Different media (twisted pair, coaxial, fiber) have vastly different characteristics affecting network performance.
